Hi Camila,

We saw a large latency regression on our nightly embedding batch that started at ~02:05 UTC and last...
text:
From: Liam O'Connell <liam.oconnell@averysys.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.ai>
Cc: support@averysys.com
Date: Tue, 1 Jul 2025 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: High latency during nightly batch - Peregrine

Hi Camila,

We saw a large latency regression on our nightly embedding batch that started at ~02:05 UTC and lasted about 1.5 hours. Average per-request p50 jumped from ~120ms to ~1.8s and several jobs timed out. This impacted our nightly model refresh and blocked a handful of downstream jobs.

Can you confirm if this was on your side and whether you can provide any credits for the impact? Our renewal is coming up and this incident materially affected our testing window.

I've attached a small excerpt of logs and job IDs. Happy to jump on a short call.

Thanks,
Liam O'Connell
Head of Platform, Avery Systems
liam.oconnell@averysys.com

Attachment: embedding-job-logs.txt (text)

Jordan —

Great chatting earlier. As we scope the MAP and the evaluation plan, ACME's staged rollout poses a few commercial questions our finance and delivery teams need covered before we finalize timelines:

1) Safety net during evaluation: can we agree on a soft-cap with automatic alerts during the first 6 weeks of ramp and a clearly priced temporary burst option if you exceed it?

2) Overage vs. emergency burst: we typically provide a two-tier approach — a 'soft cap' with no hard enforcement (alerts + throttling suggestions) and an 'emergency burst' (manual approval, higher per-token rate). We'd like to put those exact numbers...

Subject: Order form questions: renewal & termination

Hi Karthik,

Thanks for sending the draft order form. A couple of legal/procurement items came up on our side that will affect procurement signoff — can you confirm Redwood's position on the following?

1) Auto-renew term: The draft shows a 12-month auto-renew. Our procurement team prefers a rolling 6-month renewal (or a 12-month with a 60-day opt-out). Is Redwood able to accommodate either of these?

2) Notice window for non-renewal: We typically require a 60-day notice to avoid auto-renew. The order form language currently says 30 days. Can Redwood match 60 days?

To summarize Edgewave's position for the contract team: because a significant portion of our traffic will be large batched inference jobs during nightly windows, we need an exception to the standard 99.95% uptime baseline — operationally those jobs can trigger transient host scaling events and scheduled reboots that briefly impact availability.

- a capped monthly credit at 5% of committed spend for availability-related credits (rather than uncappe...

From: Ethan Ross <ethan.ross@orchid.ai>

Cc: Orchid AI Procurement <procurement@orchid.ai>
Date: Tue, 27 May 2025 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: SLA template: uptime anchoring & latency carveouts (Orchid AI)

Hi Ava,

Thanks for the demo last week — the platform looks great. Procurement reviewed the standard SLA you shared and we have two asks before we can move to PO: 

1) Uptime anchoring. For the first 90 days of the pilot we need a provisional uptime floor at 98.0% (rather than the standard 99.5%) due to the expected burst patterns during onboarding. We understand this is temporary and would expect the baseline to re-anchor at 99.5% after the pilot.

2) Latency carveouts. Our inference workload will use scheduled large-batch jobs overnight. We need explicit carveouts for batched inference windows (daily 0100–0400 PT) so batch-induced tail latency doesn’t...
